messages.content:
To perform OCR (Optical Character Recognition) on Microsoft Azure, you can use the **Azure AI Vision Read API**, which is part of the Azure AI Services. This service allows you to extract printed and handwritten text from images and documents.

### Steps to Perform OCR Using Azure

#### 1. **Create an Azure AI Vision Resource**
First, create a [Computer Vision (Vision) resource](https://portal.azure.com/) in the Azure Portal to obtain your endpoint and API key.

#### 2. **Choose Your Method**
You can do OCR via the REST API, client SDKs (e.g., Python, C#, Java), or the Azure Portal. Below is a Python SDK example.

#### 3. **Install Required Package (Python Example)**
```bash
pip install azure-ai-vision
```

#### 4. **Sample Python Code for OCR**
```python
from azure.ai.vision import VisionClient
from azure.core.credentials import AzureKeyCredential

endpoint = "https://<your-resource-name>.cognitiveservices.azure.com/"
key = "<your-key>"

client = VisionClient(endpoint, AzureKeyCredential(key))

image_url = "https://example.com/myimage.jpg"

result = client.recognize_text(image_url, mode="Printed")

for line in result.lines:
    print(line.text)
```

#### 5. **Use the REST API (Sample Request)**
```http
POST https://<your-resource-name>.cognitiveservices.azure.com/vision/v3.2/read/analyze
Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key: <your-key>
Content-Type: application/json

{
    "url": "https://example.com/myimage.jpg"
}
```

#### 6. **View Results**
After submission, you can poll the operation URL returned in the header for results.

---

**Useful Documentation:**
- [Azure AI Vision Read OCR Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/computer-vision/overview-ocr)
- [Python Quickstart: OCR with Read API](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/computer-vision/quickstarts-sdk/python-sdk)

Let me know if you want a code sample in a specific language or more details!

---
**Sources:**
- [Read API overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/computer-vision/overview-ocr)
- [Read API quickstarts](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/computer-vision/quickstarts-sdk/python-sdk)
What are the recent news about Speech services?

Here are the most recent updates and news regarding Azure Speech services, based on the official Microsoft documentation:

### Key Recent Updates (as of mid-2024):

1. **Support for OpenAI Text-to-Speech Voices**
   - Azure Speech now supports OpenAI text-to-speech voices in select regions (North Central US and Sweden Central), enabling more natural and high-quality speech synthesis.
   - Learn more: [What are OpenAI text to speech voices?](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/openai-voices)

2. **Personal Voice Enhancements**
   - The personal voice feature now supports `DragonLatestNeural` and `PhoenixLatestNeural` models for even more natural synthesized voices.
   - More info: [Integrate personal voice](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/personal-voice-how-to-use#integrate-personal-voice-in-your-application)

3. **Speech SDK Updates**
   - **SDK v1.47 (November 2025):**
     - Windows 32-bit support is dropped.
     - Several bug fixes, including a libxml2 vulnerability fix and improved behavior for special cases in text-to-speech and recognition.
     - Intent recognition and speaker recognition samples have been removed due to their retirement.
   - **SDK v1.37.0 (April 2024):**
     - Input text streaming for speech synthesis.
     - Default voice changed to en-US-AvaMultilingualNeural.
     - Various platform-specific improvements and bug fixes.
   - [View full release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/releasenotes)

4. **Service and Feature Changes**
   - **Content assessment** via Speech SDK retired (July 2025). Use Azure OpenAI models for content assessment instead.
   - **Intent recognition samples** and **speaker recognition samples** removed as the features have been retired.

5. **Improved Voice Quality and Features**
   - Neural voices are now used by default when synthesizing speech unless a specific model is specified.
   - Added a `Gender` property to make it easier to select voices.
   - Enhanced support for speaker profile management APIs.
   - Improved support for robust audio streaming and word boundary detection in speech synthesis.

6. **Batch and Real-Time Transcription Improvements**
   - Support for real-time diarization (speaker identification within conversation transcripts) using the latest Speech SDKs (since v1.31.0).

---

### More Information:
- [Official Release Notes – Azure Speech Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/releasenotes)
- [Azure Speech Service Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/)

Let me know if you want specific details on a feature or need an update summarized further!

---
**Sources:**  
- [What’s new in Azure Speech](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/releasenotes)  
- [Text-to-Speech with OpenAI Voices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/speech-service/openai-voices)

To perform geocoding (converting addresses or place names into geographic coordinates) using Microsoft Azure, you can use the Azure Maps Geocoding service.

---

## How to Do Geocoding with Azure Maps

### 1. Create an Azure Maps Account
- Go to the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com/) and create an Azure Maps account to obtain your subscription key.

### 2. Use the Search Service for Address Geocoding

You can perform geocoding using the Azure Maps REST API or an SDK like JavaScript.

#### Example: Geocoding with REST API

```http
GET https://atlas.microsoft.com/search/address/json
    ?api-version=1.0
    &subscription-key={Your-Azure-Maps-Subscription-Key}
    &query=1%20Microsoft%20Way%20Redmond%20WA
```

**Response Sample:**
This response includes the latitude and longitude for the supplied address.

#### Example: Geocoding with Azure Maps JavaScript SDK

```javascript
atlas.service.searchAddress({
    query: '1 Microsoft Way Redmond WA'
}).then(response => {
    console.log(response.results[0].position); // {lat: ..., lon: ...}
});
```
*Make sure to include and authenticate with the Azure Maps SDK.*

### 3. Use Reverse Geocoding (coordinates to address)
Similarly, you can convert coordinates back to addresses using the reverse search endpoint:

```http
GET https://atlas.microsoft.com/search/address/reverse/json
    ?api-version=1.0
    &subscription-key={Your-Azure-Maps-Subscription-Key}
    &query=47.64054,-122.12934
```

---

## Documentation & References

- [Azure Maps Address Geocoding REST API](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/maps/search/get-search-address)
- [Azure Maps SDK – JavaScript Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-maps/how-to-use-map-control)
- [Reverse Geocoding](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/maps/search/get-search-address-reverse)

Let me know if you need a code sample in a specific language or more details about integration!

---
**Sources:**  
- [Search for an address (Azure Maps)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/maps/search/get-search-address)  
- [Azure Maps documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-maps/)

Voici la traduction en français de ma précédente réponse sur la géocodification avec Azure Maps :

---

Pour effectuer de la géocodification (conversion d'adresses ou de noms de lieux en coordonnées géographiques) avec Microsoft Azure, vous pouvez utiliser le service de géocodification Azure Maps.

---

## Comment faire de la géocodification avec Azure Maps

### 1. Créez un compte Azure Maps
- Allez sur le [Portail Azure](https://portal.azure.com/) et créez un compte Azure Maps afin d'obtenir votre clé d'abonnement.

### 2. Utilisez le service de recherche pour la géocodification d'adresses

Vous pouvez réaliser la géocodification via l’API REST Azure Maps ou un SDK, comme JavaScript.

#### Exemple : Géocodification avec l’API REST

```http
GET https://atlas.microsoft.com/search/address/json
    ?api-version=1.0
    &subscription-key={Votre-Clé-D’Abonnement-Azure-Maps}
    &query=1%20Microsoft%20Way%20Redmond%20WA
```

**Exemple de réponse :**
La réponse inclura la latitude et la longitude correspondant à l'adresse fournie.

#### Exemple : Géocodification avec le SDK JavaScript Azure Maps

```javascript
atlas.service.searchAddress({
    query: '1 Microsoft Way Redmond WA'
}).then(response => {
    console.log(response.results[0].position); // {lat: ..., lon: ...}
});
```
*Assurez-vous d'inclure et d'authentifier le SDK Azure Maps.*

### 3. Utilisez la géocodification inverse (coordonnées vers adresse)

Vous pouvez également transformer des coordonnées en adresses à l’aide de l’endpoint de recherche inversée :

```http
GET https://atlas.microsoft.com/search/address/reverse/json
    ?api-version=1.0
    &subscription-key={Votre-Clé-D’Abonnement-Azure-Maps}
    &query=47.64054,-122.12934
```

---

## Documentation & Références

- [API REST Azure Maps de géocodification d’adresse](https://learn.microsoft.com/fr-fr/rest/api/maps/search/get-search-address)
- [Guide SDK JavaScript Azure Maps](https://learn.microsoft.com/fr-fr/azure/azure-maps/how-to-use-map-control)
- [Géocodification inverse](https://learn.microsoft.com/fr-fr/rest/api/maps/search/get-search-address-reverse)

Indiquez-moi si vous souhaitez un exemple de code dans un langage particulier ou plus de détails sur l'intégration !

---

**Sources** :  
- [Rechercher une adresse (Azure Maps)](https://learn.microsoft.com/fr-fr/rest/api/maps/search/get-search-address)  
- [Documentation Azure Maps](https://learn.microsoft.com/fr-fr/azure/azure-maps/)

---

Les liens pointent vers la documentation officielle Microsoft Learn en français lorsque possible.

Here is some general pricing information for Azure Maps Geocoding services:

---

## Azure Maps Geocoding Pricing

Azure Maps geocoding (both standard and batch operations) is billed based on the number of transactions ("transactions" typically mean an API call).

### Standard Pricing (as of June 2024)
- **Geocoding (Search Address/POI)**: 
  - The price starts at approximately $4.00 USD per 1,000 transactions for the S1 pricing tier. 
- **Reverse Geocoding**: 
  - Similar pricing applies: ~$4.00 USD per 1,000 transactions.
- **Free Tier**:
  - 250,000 free transactions per calendar month (shared across all Maps REST Search APIs in your account).

### Pricing Notes
- Prices can vary by region.
- Additional usage (beyond the free tier) is charged per 1,000 transactions.
- Batch geocoding and more advanced features (e.g., matrix routing) may have separate or additional costs.
- For API usage, each request (e.g., one address lookup) consumes one transaction.

---

## Price Calculator and Documentation

- You can estimate your costs using the [Azure Maps Pricing Calculator](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/calculator/?service=azure-maps).
- For full, up-to-date details, see the official Azure Maps pricing page:
  - [Azure Maps Pricing Details](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/azure-maps/)

---

Please be aware that prices and free allowances may change, so always check the official Azure pricing page for the latest information. If you need cost details for a different Azure geospatial service or a specific usage scenario, let me know!

---

**Sources:**  
- [Azure Maps Pricing Details](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/azure-maps/)  
- [Azure Maps documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-maps/)  
- [Azure Maps Address Geocoding](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/maps/search/get-search-address)
Hello! How can I assist you with Microsoft Azure or any related technology today? If you have questions or need guidance, feel free to ask!

Azure Fabric typically refers to Azure Service Fabric, which is a distributed systems platform used to build scalable, reliable, and easily managed applications for the cloud and on-premises environments.

### Key Features of Azure Service Fabric:
- **Microservices Platform:** Supports building and managing microservices and container-based applications.
- **Scalability and Reliability:** Provides automatic scaling, rolling upgrades, and self-healing.
- **Orchestration:** Manages the placement, deployment, and resiliency of services.
- **Programming Models:** Supports stateless and stateful services, containers, and various programming languages like .NET and Java.
- **DevOps Integration:** Supports DevOps processes with rolling upgrades, diagnostics, and monitoring.

### Use Cases
- Large-scale systems (e.g., Azure SQL Database, Azure Cosmos DB run on Service Fabric)
- Real-time data processing
- High-availability enterprise applications

For more information, see the official documentation:  
- [What is Azure Service Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/service-fabric/service-fabric-overview)
- [Service Fabric overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/service-fabric/service-fabric-overview)
